Two of Victarion's brothers, [Euron](wiki:Euron_Greyjoy) and [Aeron](wiki:Aeron_Greyjoy), often mocked him when they were boys.(wiki:Victarion_Greyjoy.cite_ref-Radwd56.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-1) Euron later gave Victarion his first woman to make him a man.(wiki:Victarion_Greyjoy.cite_ref-Raffc29.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-5)

Victarion and his two elder brothers, [Balon](wiki:Balon_Greyjoy) and Euron, convinced their father [Quellon](wiki:Quellon_Greyjoy), [Lord of the Iron Islands](wiki:Lord_of_the_Iron_Islands), to join [Robert's Rebellion](wiki:Robert's_Rebellion), and the [ironborn](wiki:Ironborn) raided the [Reach](wiki:Reach) late in the war. After Quellon was killed in a [battle at the Mander](wiki:Battle_at_the_Mander), however, the new Lord Balon returned to the [Iron Islands](wiki:Iron_Islands) with his younger brothers.(wiki:Victarion_Greyjoy.cite_ref-Raffc1.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_8-1)(wiki:Victarion_Greyjoy.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_iron_islands:_the_old_way_and_the_new.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_15-0)

Victarion's first wife died in childbirth, birthing a stillborn daughter, and his second wife was stricken by a pox.(wiki:Victarion_Greyjoy.cite_ref-Raffc18.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-11)

Victarion recognized the greatness of Balon and followed him loyally. He commanded the great fleet constructed by Balon, the [Iron Fleet](wiki:Iron_Fleet), during [Greyjoy's Rebellion](wiki:Greyjoy's_Rebellion). During the war, using a plan Euron had concocted, they sailed into [Lannisport](wiki:Lannisport) and [burned the Lannister fleet](wiki:Burning_of_the_Lannister_fleet) at anchor.(wiki:Victarion_Greyjoy.cite_ref-Racok24.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_12-1) Victarion tossed the first torch onto the flagship of Lord [Tywin Lannister](wiki:Tywin_Lannister).(wiki:Victarion_Greyjoy.cite_ref-Raffc18.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-12) Later in the rebellion, the Iron Fleet was smashed by [Stannis Baratheon](wiki:Stannis_Baratheon) in a [sea battle off Fair Isle](wiki:Sea_battle_off_Fair_Isle), during which *[Golden Storm](wiki:Golden_Storm)*, the vessel of Victarion's younger brother Aeron, was chopped in half by Stannis's warship, *[Fury](wiki:Fury)*.(wiki:Victarion_Greyjoy.cite_ref-Raffc1.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_8-2)

Victarion continued to serve Balon faithfully as [Lord Captain of the Iron Fleet](wiki:Lord_Captain_of_the_Iron_Fleet) after the rebellion's end, and he took a [third spouse](wiki:Victarion_Greyjoy's_third_wife), a [salt wife](wiki:Salt_wife).(wiki:Victarion_Greyjoy.cite_ref-Raffc18.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-13) According to a semi-canon source,(wiki:Victarion_Greyjoy.cite_ref-Rawoiafeuron_greyjoy.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_16-0) she had an affair with Euron, however, and was impregnated by him. When Victarion confronted his brother, Euron laughed and claimed the woman was willing. To retain his honor and prevent the cuckoldry from becoming public,(wiki:Victarion_Greyjoy.cite_ref-Raffc18.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-14) a sobbing Victarion beat his love(wiki:Victarion_Greyjoy.cite_ref-Raffc29.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-6) to death and left her body on the shore for crabs to eat. Only the taboo against [kinslaying](wiki:Kinslaying) kept Victarion from doing the same to Euron.(wiki:Victarion_Greyjoy.cite_ref-Raffc18.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-15) Victarion has not touched a woman since, nor has he forgiven Euron,(wiki:Victarion_Greyjoy.cite_ref-Raffc18.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-16) who was forced into exile by Balon in [297 AC](wiki:297_AC).(wiki:Victarion_Greyjoy.cite_ref-Racok24.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_12-2)(wiki:Victarion_Greyjoy.cite_ref-Raffc1.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_8-3) Balon commanded that neither speak of what happened.(wiki:Victarion_Greyjoy.cite_ref-Raffc18.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-17)

27	At [Ten Towers](wiki:Ten_Towers), [Rodrik Harlaw](wiki:Rodrik_Harlaw), [Lord of Harlaw](wiki:Lord_of_Harlaw), tells Asha that the last kingsmoot was held four thousand years according to Archmaester [Haereg](wiki:Haereg) or two thousand years according to Maester [Denestan](wiki:Denestan).(wiki:Kingsmoot_on_Old_Wyk.cite_ref-Raffc11.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-2) Rodrik the Reader does not want to sail to Old Wyk, but he ultimately agrees to in order to support his niece in her claim.(wiki:Kingsmoot_on_Old_Wyk.cite_ref-Raffc18.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-0)(wiki:Kingsmoot_on_Old_Wyk.cite_ref-Raffc19.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_12-0) Asha spends four days meeting with ironborn as they arrive at Old Wyk and realizes her support is not as strong as she needs.(wiki:Kingsmoot_on_Old_Wyk.cite_ref-Raffc10.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_13-0)

[Balon Greyjoy](wiki:Balon_Greyjoy), [Lord of the Iron Islands](wiki:Lord_of_the_Iron_Islands), seeks independence for the [Iron Islands](wiki:Iron_Islands) during the [War of the Five Kings](wiki:War_of_the_Five_Kings). Declaring himself [King of the Iron Islands](wiki:King_of_the_Iron_Islands),(wiki:Kingsmoot_on_Old_Wyk.cite_ref-Racok46.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-0) Balon sends the [ironborn](wiki:Ironborn) captains and the [Iron Fleet](wiki:Iron_Fleet) to attack the [north](wiki:North), as [Robb Stark](wiki:Robb_Stark), [King in the North](wiki:King_in_the_North), is away fighting in southern [Westeros](wiki:Westeros).(wiki:Kingsmoot_on_Old_Wyk.cite_ref-Racok24.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-0) Balon styles himself [King of the Isles and the North](wiki:King_of_the_Isles_and_the_North) after parts of the north are conquered by his captains.(wiki:Kingsmoot_on_Old_Wyk.cite_ref-Rasosappendix.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-0)
[Theon Greyjoy](wiki:Theon_Greyjoy), Balon's surviving son, styles himself [Prince of Winterfell](wiki:Prince_of_Winterfell),(wiki:Kingsmoot_on_Old_Wyk.cite_ref-Racok46.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-1) but he disappears after the [sack of Winterfell](wiki:Sack_of_Winterfell).(wiki:Kingsmoot_on_Old_Wyk.cite_ref-Racok66.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_5-0) Theon is feared dead after his sister, [Asha Greyjoy](wiki:Asha_Greyjoy), unsuccessfully searches for him in the ruins of Winterfell.(wiki:Kingsmoot_on_Old_Wyk.cite_ref-Raffc11.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-0) Unbeknownst to [House Greyjoy](wiki:House_Greyjoy), Theon has been taken by [Ramsay Snow](wiki:Ramsay_Snow) to the dungeons of the [Dreadfort](wiki:Dreadfort).(wiki:Kingsmoot_on_Old_Wyk.cite_ref-Radwd12.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-0)
King Balon falls to his death while crossing one of the bridges of [Pyke](wiki:Pyke).(wiki:Kingsmoot_on_Old_Wyk.cite_ref-Rasos45.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_8-0) Shortly afterward, his younger brother, [Euron Greyjoy](wiki:Euron_Greyjoy), returns from exile and claims the [Seastone Chair](wiki:Seastone_Chair) in Pyke. Lord [Sawane Botley](wiki:Sawane_Botley) objects to Euron's actions, so Euron has Sawane drowned in a cask of seawater.(wiki:Kingsmoot_on_Old_Wyk.cite_ref-Rasos45.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_8-1) The [Myraham](wiki:Myraham) escapes from [Lordsport](wiki:Lordsport) and returns to the mainland. After hearing of Balon's death from the [ship's captain](wiki:Captain_of_the_Myraham), Robb Stark plans a [siege of Moat Cailin](wiki:Siege_of_Moat_Cailin).(wiki:Kingsmoot_on_Old_Wyk.cite_ref-Rasos45.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_8-2)
Balon's youngest brother, [Aeron Greyjoy](wiki:Aeron_Greyjoy), learns of the king's death while initiating [drowned men](wiki:Drowned_men) on [Great Wyk](wiki:Great_Wyk). Considering Euron to be a godless man unworthy of sitting the Seastone Chair, Aeron Damphair calls for a [kingsmoot](wiki:Kingsmoot) to choose the new king to be held on the holy isle of [Old Wyk](wiki:Old_Wyk),(wiki:Kingsmoot_on_Old_Wyk.cite_ref-Raffc1.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_9-0) as [Nagga's Hill](wiki:Nagga's_Hill) was where the [High Kings of the Iron Islands](wiki:High_Kings_of_the_Iron_Islands) were chosen in antiquity.(wiki:Kingsmoot_on_Old_Wyk.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_iron_islands:_driftwood_crowns.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_10-0) Aeron's fellow [priests of the Drowned God](wiki:Priests_of_the_Drowned_God) spread word across the Iron Islands.(wiki:Kingsmoot_on_Old_Wyk.cite_ref-Raffc11.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-1)
At [Ten Towers](wiki:Ten_Towers), [Rodrik Harlaw](wiki:Rodrik_Harlaw), [Lord of Harlaw](wiki:Lord_of_Harlaw), tells Asha that the last kingsmoot was held four thousand years according to Archmaester [Haereg](wiki:Haereg) or two thousand years according to Maester [Denestan](wiki:Denestan).(wiki:Kingsmoot_on_Old_Wyk.cite_ref-Raffc11.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-2) Rodrik the Reader does not want to sail to Old Wyk, but he ultimately agrees to in order to support his niece in her claim.(wiki:Kingsmoot_on_Old_Wyk.cite_ref-Raffc18.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-0)(wiki:Kingsmoot_on_Old_Wyk.cite_ref-Raffc19.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_12-0) Asha spends four days meeting with ironborn as they arrive at Old Wyk and realizes her support is not as strong as she needs.(wiki:Kingsmoot_on_Old_Wyk.cite_ref-Raffc10.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_13-0)
[Victarion Greyjoy](wiki:Victarion_Greyjoy) sails to [Nagga's Cradle](wiki:Nagga's_Cradle) with most of the [Iron Fleet](wiki:Iron_Fleet), leaving a small force behind at the [Fever River](wiki:Fever_River).(wiki:Kingsmoot_on_Old_Wyk.cite_ref-Raffc18.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-1) Men from every isle have sailed to Old Wyk for the kingsmoot; Victarion sees a multitude of ships along the strand. Victarion provides a feast for half a hundred famous captains on the night before the kingsmoot, with roasted kid, salted [cod](wiki:Cod), [lobster](wiki:Lobster), and [ale](wiki:Ale) served to the guests. Asha offers to share rule with her uncle and serve as his [Hand of the King](wiki:Hand_of_the_King), but Victarion declines.(wiki:Kingsmoot_on_Old_Wyk.cite_ref-Raffc18.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-2)

### Origin[ ]

In antiquity each of the [Iron Islands](wiki:Iron_Islands) was ruled by two petty kings, a [rock king](wiki:Rock_king) who ruled the land and a [salt king](wiki:Salt_king) who led that island's ships, with each chosen by the island's captains through a [kingsmoot](wiki:Kingsmoot).(wiki:King_of_the_Iron_Islands.cite_ref-Ragotappendix.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-0) When [Galon Whitestaff](wiki:Galon_Whitestaff) called all of the minor kings and captains to a kingsmoot at [Nagga's Hill](wiki:Nagga's_Hill) on [Old Wyk](wiki:Old_Wyk), [Urras Greyiron](wiki:Urras_Greyiron) was chosen to rule all of the [ironborn](wiki:Ironborn) as the first [High King of the Iron Islands](wiki:High_King_of_the_Iron_Islands).(wiki:King_of_the_Iron_Islands.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_iron_islands:_driftwood_crowns.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-0)
After centuries of elected rulers, the kingsmoot tradition ended thousands of years ago after the controversial successions of several High Kings. [Urrathon IV Goodbrother](wiki:Urrathon_IV_Goodbrother) was chosen to succeed King [Urragon III Greyiron](wiki:Urragon_III_Greyiron) while Urragon's eldest son [Torgon](wiki:Torgon_Greyiron) was away raiding. When Torgon the Latecomer returned to the isles, the cruel Urrathon was overthrown and Torgon became High King without having been chosen in a kingsmoot. King Torgon was succeeded by his own son and advisor, [Urragon IV Greyiron](wiki:Urragon_IV_Greyiron), who was also chosen without a kingsmoot. [Priests of the Drowned God](wiki:Priests_of_the_Drowned_God) then insisted that a kingsmoot be held to choose Urragon's successor. [Urras Greyiron](wiki:Urras_Greyiron), Urragon's great-nephew and preferred successor, let loose his axemen during the meeting, with numerous petty kings and priests slaughtered at Old Wyk.(wiki:King_of_the_Iron_Islands.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_iron_islands:_driftwood_crowns.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-1)

### House Greyiron[ ]

Rather than as [High King of the Iron Islands](wiki:High_King_of_the_Iron_Islands), [Urras Redhand](wiki:Urras_Greyiron) styled himself simply King of the Iron Islands and made the throne hereditary within [House Greyiron](wiki:House_Greyiron). The [choosing](wiki:Choosing) of [salt](wiki:Salt_king) and [rock kings](wiki:Rock_king) in local [kingsmoots](wiki:Kingsmoot) ended, with the petty kings reduced to vassal [lords](wiki:Lord). Rather than the personalized crown of driftwood worn by each High King, the hereditary kings donned a crown of black [iron](wiki:Iron).(wiki:King_of_the_Iron_Islands.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_iron_islands:_the_iron_kings.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-1)
The Greyirons ruled the Iron Islands for a thousand years,(wiki:King_of_the_Iron_Islands.cite_ref-date_8-0) during which they faced six rebellions from rival ironborn and two uprisings by [thralls](wiki:Thrall). The ironmen also lost control of their remaining conquests from the [green lands](wiki:Green_lands), including the [Shield Islands](wiki:Shield_Islands).(wiki:King_of_the_Iron_Islands.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_iron_islands:_the_iron_kings.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-2)
